documentation.name: Acme Starter Kit
documentation.description: A minimal design system bundle containing core components, foundational tokens, a spacing style, and an error messaging pattern.

items.name: button
items.description: An interactive element that triggers an action when activated.

items.name: emphasis
items.description: Controls the visual weight of the button.

values.name: primary
values.description: High-emphasis — the main action on the surface.

values.name: secondary
values.description: Medium-emphasis — important but not the primary action.

values.name: danger
values.description: Signals a destructive or irreversible action.

items.name: size
items.description: Controls the physical dimensions of the button.

values.name: small
values.description: Compact size for toolbars and dense layouts.

values.name: medium
values.description: The default size for most contexts.

items.name: default
items.description: The resting state.

items.name: hover
items.description: Pointer is over the button. Background darkens by 8%.

items.name: disabled
items.description: Non-interactive. Opacity reduced to 40%.

items.name: loading
items.description: Label replaced by a spinner. Button is non-interactive.

whenToUse.description: When the user needs to trigger an action such as submitting a form, saving data, or opening a dialog.
When a destructive action needs to be initiated, such as deleting a record. Pair with a confirmation dialog.

whenNotToUse.description: When the action navigates to a different page or URL.

alternative.name: link
alternative.rationale: Links carry native navigation semantics. Screen readers announce them as links, and browsers support open-in-new-tab.

items.guidance: Limit each surface to one primary button.
items.rationale: Multiple primary buttons dilute visual hierarchy.

items.guidance: Use the loading state instead of disabling the button during async operations.
items.rationale: A disabled button gives no feedback that an action is in progress.

items.guidance: When using an icon-only button, provide an aria-label.
items.rationale: Screen readers need a text equivalent for every interactive control.

items.name: link
items.description: A navigational element that takes the user to a different page or URL.

whenToUse.description: When the action navigates the user to a different page, section, or external resource.

whenNotToUse.description: When the action triggers a state change or submits data without navigation.

alternative.name: button
alternative.rationale: Buttons have action semantics. Using a link for non-navigation actions creates a mismatch between role and behavior.

items.guidance: Always pair link color with an underline for inline links within body text.
items.rationale: Color alone is insufficient to distinguish links for users with color vision deficiency.

items.name: alert
items.description: A message bar that communicates status, errors, warnings, or informational messages to the user.

items.name: severity
items.description: The visual treatment and semantic meaning of the alert.

values.name: warning
values.description: Warns about a potential issue that doesn't block the user.

values.name: error
values.description: Communicates an error that requires attention.

items.guidance: Use role='alert' or aria-live='assertive' for error alerts so screen readers announce them immediately.
items.rationale: Without a live region, dynamically injected error messages are invisible to assistive technology.

items.name: color-action-primary
items.description: The primary interactive color. Used for primary buttons and key interactive elements.

items.name: color-action-danger
items.description: The danger interactive color. Used for destructive actions.

items.name: color-text-on-action
items.description: Text color for use on filled interactive surfaces (primary buttons, danger buttons).

items.name: spacing
items.description: A spatial system built on a 4px base unit for consistent whitespace across all surfaces.

items.title: Use the scale
items.description: Every spacing value must reference a token from the spacing scale. No hard-coded pixel values.

items.title: Density over decoration
items.description: Spacing exists to create clear relationships between elements, not to fill empty areas.

references.name: spacing
references.role: Provides all spatial tokens from space-0 through space-8.

guidelines.name: spacing-scale
guidelines.description: A geometric scale based on a 4px unit.

items.guidance: Components must not apply external margin. Margin between components is the responsibility of the parent layout.
items.rationale: Components that own their margins create unpredictable spacing when composed.

items.guidance: Use CSS gap as the primary mechanism for spacing between sibling elements.
items.rationale: Gap applies spacing uniformly without affecting the first or last child.

items.name: error-messaging
items.description: How errors are surfaced through inline validation, summary banners, and toast notifications.

references.name: alert
references.role: Page-level error summary listing all current errors.

references.name: button
references.role: Submit trigger that initiates client-side validation.

items.description: Client-side validation runs on all required fields. If any fail, submission is prevented.
Each invalid field displays an inline error message. An error summary alert appears at the top of the form with anchor links to each field.
The inline error for that field is removed. When all errors are resolved, the summary disappears.

whenToUse.description: When a form submission fails validation and the user needs to correct one or more fields.

whenNotToUse.description: When the feedback is a successful outcome.

alternative.name: success-messaging
alternative.rationale: Success feedback uses success colors and icons. Reusing the error pattern for positive outcomes confuses the visual language.

items.guidance: Always display inline errors directly below the invalid field.
items.rationale: Proximity eliminates the cognitive load of mapping an error message to its field.

items.guidance: Move focus to the error summary when it appears.
items.rationale: Screen reader users will not discover the summary unless focus is moved to it.

items.guidance: Do not clear the form or reset field values when validation fails.
items.rationale: Clearing the form destroys the user's work.
